title: Mood Manipulation and PST, Experiment 2
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 50 healthy control participants performing a reinforcement learning task under two mood conditions (sad and happy mood manipulation). The study examines how affective state influences learning and decision-making processes, with data collected between 2019-2021. The task includes distinct training and testing phases, with mood manipulation administered prior to each training block.
methods_description: EEG data were collected from 50 healthy controls (25 following sad mood manipulation, 25 following happy mood manipulation) during a reinforcement learning task adapted from Daw et al. (2006). The task included training and testing sections with mood manipulation administered before each training block. Data collection occurred at the Cognitive Rhythms and Computation Lab at the University of New Mexico between 2019-2021.
